title: The Gateless Barrier: The Wu-Men Kuan (Mumonkan)
strategy: case-by-case extraction; use case title, core case, Wu-men verse, and avoid long commentary excerpts
whyNow: Clean table of 48 cases, good OCR, each case is already artifact-shaped.
copyrightRisk: medium-high; use short excerpts and working renderings only

title: Psalms canonical source
strategy: extract short Hebrew/English/Spanish verse-level artifacts from canonical public references
whyNow: 150 psalms, highly compact verse units, public canonical anchors available.
copyrightRisk: low when using public-domain / open references

title: The Book of Psalms, KJV Fall River Press edition
strategy: use as local reading witness for KJV phrasing; source artifacts to public KJV/Hebrew references before promotion
whyNow: Good OCR and obvious verse boundaries; useful for fast candidate selection.
copyrightRisk: low-medium; KJV text public domain, but edition design is copyrighted

title: Dao De Jing canonical Chinese source
strategy: chapter-by-chapter extraction from Chinese text; pair with short working English/Spanish renderings
whyNow: 81 compact chapters; stable public Chinese reference.
copyrightRisk: low when translations are working renderings

title: Dao De Jing: Ancient Immortal's Theory of Everything
strategy: use as local comparative witness only; prefer original Chinese and independent working renderings for promoted rows
whyNow: Good OCR and line-by-line Chinese/English structure, but modern copyrighted interpretation is idiosyncratic.
copyrightRisk: high; do not promote long translated passages from this edition

title: The Sayings of the Desert Fathers
strategy: numbered saying extraction; one apophthegm per candidate; short Spanish working rendering
whyNow: Extremely clean unit structure and high contemplative density.
copyrightRisk: medium; use short excerpts and page locators

title: Poopol Wuuj / Popol Vuh
strategy: bilingual K'ichee'-Spanish paired extraction; English as working rendering only
whyNow: Strong Americas source; bilingual page structure is ideal for original/Spanish columns.
copyrightRisk: medium; use short fragments with page locators

title: Japanese Death Poems
strategy: poem-by-poem extraction; original if available; preserve poet and page locator

title: Masnavi i Ma'navi / Teachings of Rumi, Whinfield
strategy: extract short couplets only; reject narrative summaries unless they compress into a single contemplative pivot
whyNow: Public-domain translation and good OCR; high yield if constrained to couplets.
copyrightRisk: low for translation text; avoid layout reuse

title: The Poems of Hafez, Reza Saberi translation
strategy: ghazal-by-ghazal candidate selection; prefer one or two short couplets per ghazal; add Persian original only from reliable public Persian source before verification
whyNow: Strong poetic density but image-only/OCR-poor and copyrighted; better after the cleaner Sufi source proves the flow.
copyrightRisk: high; sparse excerpting, strong provenance, review required

title: Greek Anthology
strategy: extract epitaphs and short votive/admonitory epigrams only; reject ornamental long pieces
whyNow: Very high density, but requires careful filtering and Greek/English pairing.

title: Principal Upanishads / Sacred Books of the East
strategy: extract mahavakya-like short teachings; Sanskrit source required where possible; Spanish as working rendering
whyNow: Dense metaphysical formulas; public-domain anchors available.
copyrightRisk: low when using public-domain Max Müller / Sacred Texts

title: Cantares Mexicanos
strategy: OCR/manual image review; extract Nahuatl/English/Spanish only when source alignment is reliable
whyNow: Very important, but scan/OCR complexity makes it slower.

title: Voces / Voices, Antonio Porchia
strategy: very selective aphorism extraction; original Spanish only when legible; use short excerpts and mark translation provenance
whyNow: Excellent fit but image-only and copyrighted; should be sparse and careful.

title: Zhuangzi: Ways of Wandering the Way
strategy: use for conceptual guidance and source discovery, not direct artifact extraction unless the source passage is short and independently anchored
whyNow: Philosophically valuable but commentary-heavy and copyrighted.
